text:
There was an old owl who loved cookies. Every night, she would swoop down into her favorite oak tree and catch some. There was a squirrel who loved cookies too. Every morning, she’d fly up and steal the old owl’s.

One night, the owl woke up furious. It wasn’t them. She hooted terribly. She flew straight down.

The squirrel didn’t know what to do.

Just then a fox came hopping along.

"Oh dear,” she said. “That owl never complains.”

You? You want that?

The fox just smiled.

That year you took all the acorns. I was grateful.

That winter you starved. Even rabbits didn’t like you.

But you. Hmm?

That fox...

Well. There’s a badger that goes running.

Now?Oh.
